title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement mrowlan1@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: gutenbergr: Search and download public domain texts from Project Gutenberg
text: package contains metadata Project Gutenberg works R datasets, can search filter particular works downloading. dataset gutenberg_metadata contains information work, pairing Gutenberg ID title, author, language, etc: example, find Gutenberg ID(s) Jane Austen’s Persuasion : many analyses, may want filter just English works, avoid duplicates, include books text can downloaded. gutenberg_works() function pre-filtering: also allows perform filtering argument: meta-data currently package last updated 29 November 2023.
code: library(gutenbergr) library(dplyr) gutenberg_metadata #> # A tibble: 75,473 × 8 #>    gutenberg_id title    author gutenberg_author_id language gutenberg_bookshelf #>           <int> <chr>    <chr>                <int> <chr>    <chr>               #>  1            1 "The De… Jeffe…                1638 en       "Politics/American… #>  2            2 "The Un… Unite…                   1 en       "Politics/American… #>  3            3 "John F… Kenne…                1666 en       ""                  #>  4            4 "Lincol… Linco…                   3 en       "US Civil War"      #>  5            5 "The Un… Unite…                   1 en       "United States/Pol… #>  6            6 "Give M… Henry…                   4 en       "American Revoluti… #>  7            7 "The Ma… NA                      NA en       ""                  #>  8            8 "Abraha… Linco…                   3 en       "US Civil War"      #>  9            9 "Abraha… Linco…                   3 en       "US Civil War"      #> 10           10 "The Ki… NA                      NA en       "Banned Books List… #> # ℹ 75,463 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: rights <chr>, has_text <lgl> gutenberg_metadata %>%   filter(title == "Persuasion") #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   gutenberg_id title     author gutenberg_author_id language gutenberg_bookshelf #>          <int> <chr>     <chr>                <int> <chr>    <chr>               #> 1          105 Persuasi… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #> 2        22963 Persuasi… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #> 3        36777 Persuasi… Auste…                  68 fr       "FR Littérature"    #> # ℹ 2 more variables: rights <chr>, has_text <lgl> gutenberg_works() #> # A tibble: 59,146 × 8 #>    gutenberg_id title    author gutenberg_author_id language gutenberg_bookshelf #>           <int> <chr>    <chr>                <int> <chr>    <chr>               #>  1            1 "The De… Jeffe…                1638 en       "Politics/American… #>  2            2 "The Un… Unite…                   1 en       "Politics/American… #>  3            3 "John F… Kenne…                1666 en       ""                  #>  4            4 "Lincol… Linco…                   3 en       "US Civil War"      #>  5            5 "The Un… Unite…                   1 en       "United States/Pol… #>  6            6 "Give M… Henry…                   4 en       "American Revoluti… #>  7            7 "The Ma… NA                      NA en       ""                  #>  8            8 "Abraha… Linco…                   3 en       "US Civil War"      #>  9            9 "Abraha… Linco…                   3 en       "US Civil War"      #> 10           10 "The Ki… NA                      NA en       "Banned Books List… #> # ℹ 59,136 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: rights <chr>, has_text <lgl> gutenberg_works(author == "Austen, Jane") #> # A tibble: 12 × 8 #>    gutenberg_id title    author gutenberg_author_id language gutenberg_bookshelf #>           <int> <chr>    <chr>                <int> <chr>    <chr>               #>  1          105 "Persua… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  2          121 "Northa… Auste…                  68 en       "Gothic Fiction"    #>  3          141 "Mansfi… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  4          158 "Emma"   Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  5          161 "Sense … Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  6          946 "Lady S… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  7         1212 "Love a… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  8         1342 "Pride … Auste…                  68 en       "Best Books Ever L… #>  9        31100 "The Co… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #> 10        37431 "Pride … Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #> 11        42078 "The Le… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #> 12        63569 "The Wa… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #> # ℹ 2 more variables: rights <chr>, has_text <lgl>  # or with a regular expression  library(stringr) gutenberg_works(str_detect(author, "Austen")) #> # A tibble: 22 × 8 #>    gutenberg_id title    author gutenberg_author_id language gutenberg_bookshelf #>           <int> <chr>    <chr>                <int> <chr>    <chr>               #>  1          105 Persuas… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  2          121 Northan… Auste…                  68 en       "Gothic Fiction"    #>  3          141 Mansfie… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  4          158 Emma     Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  5          161 Sense a… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  6          946 Lady Su… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  7         1212 Love an… Auste…                  68 en       ""                  #>  8         1342 Pride a… Auste…                  68 en       "Best Books Ever L… #>  9        17797 Memoir … Auste…                7603 en       ""                  #> 10        22536 Jane Au… Auste…               25392 en       ""                  #> # ℹ 12 more rows #> # ℹ 2 more variables: rights <chr>, has_text <lgl>

title: gutenbergr: Search and download public domain texts from Project Gutenberg
text: function gutenberg_download() downloads one works Project Gutenberg based ID. example, earlier saw one version Persuasion ID 105 (see URL ), gutenberg_download(105) downloads text. Notice returned tbl_df (type data frame) including two variables: gutenberg_id (useful multiple books returned), character vector text, one row per line. can also provide gutenberg_download() vector IDs download multiple books. example, download Renascence, Poems (book 109) along Persuasion, : Notice meta_fields argument allows us add one additional fields gutenberg_metadata downloaded text, title author.
code: persuasion <- gutenberg_download(105) persuasion #> # A tibble: 8,328 × 2 #>    gutenberg_id text          #>           <int> <chr>         #>  1          105 "Persuasion"  #>  2          105 ""            #>  3          105 ""            #>  4          105 "by"          #>  5          105 ""            #>  6          105 "Jane Austen" #>  7          105 ""            #>  8          105 "(1818)"      #>  9          105 ""            #> 10          105 ""            #> # ℹ 8,318 more rows books <- gutenberg_download(c(109, 105), meta_fields = "title") books #> # A tibble: 9,550 × 3 #>    gutenberg_id text          title      #>           <int> <chr>         <chr>      #>  1          105 "Persuasion"  Persuasion #>  2          105 ""            Persuasion #>  3          105 ""            Persuasion #>  4          105 "by"          Persuasion #>  5          105 ""            Persuasion #>  6          105 "Jane Austen" Persuasion #>  7          105 ""            Persuasion #>  8          105 "(1818)"      Persuasion #>  9          105 ""            Persuasion #> 10          105 ""            Persuasion #> # ℹ 9,540 more rows books %>%   count(title) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   title                           n #>   <chr>                       <int> #> 1 Persuasion                   8328 #> 2 Renascence, and Other Poems  1222

title: gutenbergr: Search and download public domain texts from Project Gutenberg
text: may want select books based information title author, genre topic. gutenberg_subjects contains pairings works Library Congress subjects topics. “lcc” means Library Congress Classification, “lcsh” means Library Congress subject headings: useful extracting texts particular topic genre, detective stories, particular character, Sherlock Holmes. gutenberg_id column can used download texts link metadata. gutenberg_authors contains information author, aliases birth/death year:
code: gutenberg_subjects #> # A tibble: 241,189 × 3 #>    gutenberg_id subject_type subject                                             #>           <int> <chr>        <chr>                                               #>  1            1 lcsh         United States -- History -- Revolution, 1775-1783 … #>  2            1 lcsh         United States. Declaration of Independence          #>  3            1 lcc          E201                                                #>  4            1 lcc          JK                                                  #>  5            2 lcsh         Civil rights -- United States -- Sources            #>  6            2 lcsh         United States. Constitution. 1st-10th Amendments    #>  7            2 lcc          JK                                                  #>  8            2 lcc          KF                                                  #>  9            3 lcsh         United States -- Foreign relations -- 1961-1963     #> 10            3 lcsh         Presidents -- United States -- Inaugural addresses  #> # ℹ 241,179 more rows gutenberg_subjects %>%   filter(subject == "Detective and mystery stories") #> # A tibble: 843 × 3 #>    gutenberg_id subject_type subject                       #>           <int> <chr>        <chr>                         #>  1          170 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #>  2          173 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #>  3          244 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #>  4          305 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #>  5          330 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #>  6          481 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #>  7          547 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #>  8          863 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #>  9          905 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #> 10         1155 lcsh         Detective and mystery stories #> # ℹ 833 more rows  gutenberg_subjects %>%   filter(grepl("Holmes, Sherlock", subject)) #> # A tibble: 55 × 3 #>    gutenberg_id subject_type subject                                            #>           <int> <chr>        <chr>                                              #>  1          108 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #>  2          221 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #>  3          244 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #>  4          834 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #>  5         1661 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #>  6         2097 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #>  7         2343 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #>  8         2344 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #>  9         2345 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #> 10         2346 lcsh         Holmes, Sherlock (Fictitious character) -- Fiction #> # ℹ 45 more rows gutenberg_authors #> # A tibble: 24,901 × 7 #>    gutenberg_author_id author        alias birthdate deathdate wikipedia aliases #>                  <int> <chr>         <chr>     <int>     <int> <chr>     <chr>   #>  1                   1 United States U.S.…        NA        NA https://… U.S.A.  #>  2                   3 Lincoln, Abr… NA         1809      1865 https://… United… #>  3                   4 Henry, Patri… NA         1736      1799 https://… NA      #>  4                   5 Adam, Paul    NA         1849      1931 https://… NA      #>  5                   7 Carroll, Lew… Dodg…      1832      1898 https://… Dodgso… #>  6                   8 United State… NA           NA        NA https://… Agency… #>  7                   9 Melville, He… Melv…      1819      1891 https://… Melvil… #>  8                  10 Barrie, J. M… NA         1860      1937 https://… Barrie… #>  9                  11 Church of Je… NA           NA        NA https://… NA      #> 10                  12 Smith, Josep… Smit…      1805      1844 https://… Smith,… #> # ℹ 24,891 more rows

title: gutenbergr: Search and download public domain texts from Project Gutenberg
text: ’s next retrieving book’s text? Well, book data frame especially useful working tidytext package text analysis. may also find resources useful: Natural Language Processing CRAN View suggests many R packages related text mining, especially around tm package match wikipedia column gutenberg_author Wikipedia content WikipediR package pageview statistics wikipediatrend package ’re considering analysis based author name, may find humaniformat (extraction first names) gender (prediction gender first names) packages useful. (Note humaniformat format_reverse function reversing “Last, First” names).
code: library(tidytext)  words <- books %>%   unnest_tokens(word, text)  words #> # A tibble: 90,532 × 3 #>    gutenberg_id title      word       #>           <int> <chr>      <chr>      #>  1          105 Persuasion persuasion #>  2          105 Persuasion by         #>  3          105 Persuasion jane       #>  4          105 Persuasion austen     #>  5          105 Persuasion 1818       #>  6          105 Persuasion chapter    #>  7          105 Persuasion 1          #>  8          105 Persuasion sir        #>  9          105 Persuasion walter     #> 10          105 Persuasion elliot     #> # ℹ 90,522 more rows  word_counts <- words %>%   anti_join(stop_words, by = "word") %>%   count(title, word, sort = TRUE)  word_counts #> # A tibble: 6,620 × 3 #>    title      word          n #>    <chr>      <chr>     <int> #>  1 Persuasion anne        447 #>  2 Persuasion captain     303 #>  3 Persuasion elliot      254 #>  4 Persuasion lady        214 #>  5 Persuasion wentworth   191 #>  6 Persuasion charles     155 #>  7 Persuasion time        152 #>  8 Persuasion sir         149 #>  9 Persuasion miss        125 #> 10 Persuasion walter      123 #> # ℹ 6,610 more rows

what: gutenbergr: R package to search and download public domain texts from Project Gutenberg
title: Download and Process Public Domain Works from Project Gutenberg
text: Authors: David RobinsonLicense: GPL-2 Download process public domain works Project Gutenberg collection. Includes function gutenberg_download() downloads one works Project Gutenberg ID: e.g., gutenberg_download(84) downloads text Frankenstein. gutenberg_metadata contains information work, pairing Gutenberg ID title, author, language, etc gutenberg_authors contains information author, aliases birth/death year gutenberg_subjects contains pairings works Library Congress subjects topics

previous_headings: gutenbergr: R package to search and download public domain texts from Project Gutenberg
title: Download and Process Public Domain Works from Project Gutenberg
text: Install package : install development version using devtools :
code: install.packages("gutenbergr") devtools::install_github("ropensci/gutenbergr")

previous_headings: gutenbergr: R package to search and download public domain texts from Project Gutenberg
title: Download and Process Public Domain Works from Project Gutenberg
text: gutenberg_works() function retrieves, default, table metadata unique English-language Project Gutenberg works text associated . (gutenberg_metadata dataset Gutenberg works, unfiltered). Suppose wanted download Emily Bronte’s “Wuthering Heights.” find book’s ID filtering: Since see gutenberg_id 768, can download gutenberg_download() function: gutenberg_download can download multiple books given multiple IDs. also takes meta_fields argument add variables metadata. can also take output gutenberg_works directly. example, get text Aristotle’s works, annotated gutenberg_id title, using:
code: library(dplyr) library(gutenbergr)  gutenberg_works() %>%   filter(title == "Wuthering Heights") #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   gutenberg_id title             author        gutenberg_author_id language #>          <int> <chr>             <chr>                       <int> <chr>    #> 1          768 Wuthering Heights Brontë, Emily                 405 en       #>   gutenberg_bookshelf                                 rights                    has_text #>   <chr>                                               <chr>                     <lgl>    #> 1 Best Books Ever Listings/Gothic Fiction/Movie Books Public domain in the USA. TRUE  # or just: gutenberg_works(title == "Wuthering Heights") #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   gutenberg_id title             author        gutenberg_author_id language #>          <int> <chr>             <chr>                       <int> <chr>    #> 1          768 Wuthering Heights Brontë, Emily                 405 en       #>   gutenberg_bookshelf                                 rights                    has_text #>   <chr>                                               <chr>                     <lgl>    #> 1 Best Books Ever Listings/Gothic Fiction/Movie Books Public domain in the USA. TRUE wuthering_heights <- gutenberg_download(768) wuthering_heights #> # A tibble: 12,342 × 2 #>    gutenberg_id text                #>           <int> <chr>               #>  1          768 "Wuthering Heights" #>  2          768 ""                  #>  3          768 "by Emily Brontë"   #>  4          768 ""                  #>  5          768 ""                  #>  6          768 ""                  #>  7          768 ""                  #>  8          768 "CHAPTER I"         #>  9          768 ""                  #> 10          768 ""                  #> # ℹ 12,332 more rows # 1260 is the ID of Jane Eyre books <- gutenberg_download(c(768, 1260), meta_fields = "title") books #> # A tibble: 33,343 × 3 #>    gutenberg_id text                title             #>           <int> <chr>               <chr>             #>  1          768 "Wuthering Heights" Wuthering Heights #>  2          768 ""                  Wuthering Heights #>  3          768 "by Emily Brontë"   Wuthering Heights #>  4          768 ""                  Wuthering Heights #>  5          768 ""                  Wuthering Heights #>  6          768 ""                  Wuthering Heights #>  7          768 ""                  Wuthering Heights #>  8          768 "CHAPTER I"         Wuthering Heights #>  9          768 ""                  Wuthering Heights #> 10          768 ""                  Wuthering Heights #> # ℹ 33,333 more rows  books %>%   count(title) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   title                           n #>   <chr>                       <int> #> 1 Jane Eyre: An Autobiography 21001 #> 2 Wuthering Heights           12342 aristotle_books <- gutenberg_works(author == "Aristotle") %>%   gutenberg_download(meta_fields = "title")  aristotle_books #> # A tibble: 17,147 × 3 #>    gutenberg_id text                                                                     #>           <int> <chr>                                                                    #>  1         1974 "THE POETICS OF ARISTOTLE"                                               #>  2         1974 ""                                                                       #>  3         1974 "By Aristotle"                                                           #>  4         1974 ""                                                                       #>  5         1974 "A Translation By S. H. Butcher"                                         #>  6         1974 ""                                                                       #>  7         1974 ""                                                                       #>  8         1974 "[Transcriber's Annotations and Conventions: the translator left"        #>  9         1974 "intact some Greek words to illustrate a specific point of the original" #> 10         1974 "discourse. In this transcription, in order to retain the accuracy of"   #>    title                    #>    <chr>                    #>  1 The Poetics of Aristotle #>  2 The Poetics of Aristotle #>  3 The Poetics of Aristotle #>  4 The Poetics of Aristotle #>  5 The Poetics of Aristotle #>  6 The Poetics of Aristotle #>  7 The Poetics of Aristotle #>  8 The Poetics of Aristotle #>  9 The Poetics of Aristotle #> 10 The Poetics of Aristotle #> # ℹ 17,137 more rows

previous_headings: gutenbergr: R package to search and download public domain texts from Project Gutenberg > FAQ
what: Do you respect the rules regarding robot access to Project Gutenberg?
title: Download and Process Public Domain Works from Project Gutenberg
text: Yes! package respects rules complies best ability. Namely: Project Gutenberg allows wget harvest Project Gutenberg using list links. gutenbergr package visits page find recommended mirror user’s location. retrieve book text directly mirror using links format. example, Frankenstein (book 84) retrieved https://www.gutenberg.lib.md.us/8/84/84.zip. retrieve .zip file rather txt minimize bandwidth mirror. Still, package right way download entire Project Gutenberg corpus (particular language). , follow recommendation use wget set mirror. package recommended downloading single work, works particular author topic.

what: Metadata about Project Gutenberg authors — gutenberg_authors
title: Metadata about Project Gutenberg authors — gutenberg_authors
text: Data frame metadata author Project Gutenberg work. Although Project Gutenberg raw data also includes metadata contributors, editors, illustrators, etc., dataset contains people single author least one work.

title: Metadata about Project Gutenberg authors — gutenberg_authors
code: gutenberg_authors

title: Metadata about Project Gutenberg authors — gutenberg_authors
text: tbl_df (see tibble dplyr) one row author, columns gutenberg_author_id Unique identifier author can   used join gutenberg_metadata dataset author agent_name field original metadata alias Alias birthdate Year birth deathdate Year death wikipedia Link Wikipedia article author.   multiple, "|"-delimited aliases Character vector aliases.   multiple, "/"-delimited

title: Metadata about Project Gutenberg authors — gutenberg_authors
text: find date metadata last updated, run attr(gutenberg_authors, "date_updated").

title: Metadata about Project Gutenberg authors — gutenberg_authors
code: # date last updated attr(gutenberg_authors, "date_updated") #> [1] "2023-11-29"

what: Download one or more works using a Project Gutenberg ID — gutenberg_download
title: Download one or more works using a Project Gutenberg ID — gutenberg_download
text: Download one works Project Gutenberg IDs data frame one row per line per work. can used download single work interest multiple time. can look Gutenberg IDs work using gutenberg_works() function gutenberg_metadata dataset.

title: Download one or more works using a Project Gutenberg ID — gutenberg_download
code: gutenberg_download(   gutenberg_id,   mirror = NULL,   strip = TRUE,   meta_fields = NULL,   verbose = TRUE,   files = NULL,   ... )

title: Download one or more works using a Project Gutenberg ID — gutenberg_download
text: gutenberg_id vector Project Gutenberg ID, data frame containing gutenberg_id column, results gutenberg_works() call mirror Optionally mirror URL retrieve books . default uses mirror gutenberg_get_mirror strip Whether strip suspected headers footers using gutenberg_strip function meta_fields Additional fields, title author, add gutenberg_metadata describing book. useful returning multiple verbose Whether show messages Project Gutenberg mirror chosen files vector .zip file paths. given, reads files rather site. mostly used testing Project Gutenberg website may available. ... Extra arguments passed gutenberg_strip, currently unused

title: Download one or more works using a Project Gutenberg ID — gutenberg_download
text: two column tbl_df (type data frame; see tibble dplyr packages) one row line text texts, columns gutenberg_id Integer column Project Gutenberg ID   text text character vector

title: Download one or more works using a Project Gutenberg ID — gutenberg_download
text: Note strip = TRUE, tries remove Gutenberg header footer using gutenberg_strip function. exact process since headers footers differ books. -depth analysis may want check start end downloaded book.

title: Download one or more works using a Project Gutenberg ID — gutenberg_download
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive()  library(dplyr)  # download The Count of Monte Cristo gutenberg_download(1184)  # download two books: Wuthering Heights and Jane Eyre books <- gutenberg_download(c(768, 1260), meta_fields = "title") books books %>% count(title)  # download all books from Jane Austen austen <- gutenberg_works(author == "Austen, Jane") %>%   gutenberg_download(meta_fields = "title")  austen austen %>%   count(title)  }

what: Gutenberg metadata about each work — gutenberg_metadata
title: Gutenberg metadata about each work — gutenberg_metadata
text: Selected fields metadata Project Gutenberg works. collected using gitenberg Python package, particularly pg_rdf_to_json function.

title: Gutenberg metadata about each work — gutenberg_metadata
code: gutenberg_metadata

title: Gutenberg metadata about each work — gutenberg_metadata
text: tbl_df (see tibble dplyr) one row work Project   Gutenberg following columns: gutenberg_id Numeric ID, used retrieve works   Project Gutenberg title Title author Author, single one given. Given last name   first (e.g. "Doyle, Arthur Conan") author_id Project Gutenberg author ID language Language ISO 639 code, separated / multiple. Two   letter code one exists, otherwise three letter. See   https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-2_codes gutenberg_bookshelf collection collections   found , separated / multiple rights Generally one three options: "Public domain USA."   (common far), "Copyrighted. Read copyright notice inside   book details.", "None" has_text Whether file containing digits followed   .txt Project Gutenberg record (opposed ,   example, audiobooks). , retrieved   gutenberg_download

title: Gutenberg metadata about each work — gutenberg_metadata
text: find date metadata last updated, run   attr(gutenberg_metadata, "date_updated").

title: Gutenberg metadata about each work — gutenberg_metadata
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive()  library(dplyr) library(stringr)  gutenberg_metadata  gutenberg_metadata %>%   count(author, sort = TRUE)  # look for Shakespeare, excluding collections (containing "Works") and # translations shakespeare_metadata <- gutenberg_metadata %>%   filter(     author == "Shakespeare, William",     language == "en",     !str_detect(title, "Works"),     has_text,     !str_detect(rights, "Copyright")   ) %>%   distinct(title)  # \donttest{ shakespeare_works <- gutenberg_download(shakespeare_metadata$gutenberg_id) # }  # note that the gutenberg_works() function filters for English # non-copyrighted works and does de-duplication by default:  shakespeare_metadata2 <- gutenberg_works(   author == "Shakespeare, William",   !str_detect(title, "Works") )  # date last updated attr(gutenberg_metadata, "date_updated") }

what: Strip header and footer content from a Project Gutenberg book — gutenberg_strip
title: Strip header and footer content from a Project Gutenberg book — gutenberg_strip
text: Strip header footer content Project Gutenberg book. based formatting guesses may perfect. also strip tables contents, prologues, text appears start book.

title: Strip header and footer content from a Project Gutenberg book — gutenberg_strip
code: gutenberg_strip(text)

title: Strip header and footer content from a Project Gutenberg book — gutenberg_strip
text: text character vector lines book

title: Strip header and footer content from a Project Gutenberg book — gutenberg_strip
text: character vector Project Gutenberg headers footers removed

title: Strip header and footer content from a Project Gutenberg book — gutenberg_strip
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive()  library(dplyr) book <- gutenberg_works(title == "Pride and Prejudice") %>%   gutenberg_download(strip = FALSE)  head(book$text, 10) tail(book$text, 10)  text_stripped <- gutenberg_strip(book$text)  head(text_stripped, 10) tail(text_stripped, 10)  }

what: Gutenberg metadata about the subject of each work — gutenberg_subjects
title: Gutenberg metadata about the subject of each work — gutenberg_subjects
text: Gutenberg metadata subject work, particularly Library Congress Classifications (lcc) Library Congress Subject Headings (lcsh).

title: Gutenberg metadata about the subject of each work — gutenberg_subjects
code: gutenberg_subjects

title: Gutenberg metadata about the subject of each work — gutenberg_subjects
text: tbl_df (see tibble dplyr) one row pairing work subject, columns: gutenberg_id ID describing work can joined   gutenberg_metadata subject_type Either "lcc" (Library Congress Classification)   "lcsh" (Library Congress Subject Headings) subject Subject

title: Gutenberg metadata about the subject of each work — gutenberg_subjects
text: Find information Library Congress Categories : https://www.loc.gov/catdir/cpso/lcco/, Library Congress Subject Headings : https://id.loc.gov/authorities/subjects.html. find date metadata last updated, run attr(gutenberg_subjects, "date_updated").

title: Gutenberg metadata about the subject of each work — gutenberg_subjects
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive()  library(dplyr) library(stringr)  gutenberg_subjects %>%   filter(subject_type == "lcsh") %>%   count(subject, sort = TRUE)  sherlock_holmes_subjects <- gutenberg_subjects %>%   filter(str_detect(subject, "Holmes, Sherlock"))  sherlock_holmes_subjects  sherlock_holmes_metadata <- gutenberg_works() %>%   filter(author == "Doyle, Arthur Conan") %>%   semi_join(sherlock_holmes_subjects, by = "gutenberg_id")  sherlock_holmes_metadata  # \donttest{ holmes_books <- gutenberg_download(sherlock_holmes_metadata$gutenberg_id)  holmes_books # }  # date last updated attr(gutenberg_subjects, "date_updated") }

what: Get a filtered table of Gutenberg work metadata — gutenberg_works
title: Get a filtered table of Gutenberg work metadata — gutenberg_works
text: Get table Gutenberg work metadata filtered common (settable) defaults, along option add additional filters. function convenience working common conditions pulling set books analyze. detailed filtering entire Project Gutenberg metadata, use gutenberg_metadata related datasets.

title: Get a filtered table of Gutenberg work metadata — gutenberg_works
code: gutenberg_works(   ...,   languages = "en",   only_text = TRUE,   rights = c("Public domain in the USA.", "None"),   distinct = TRUE,   all_languages = FALSE,   only_languages = TRUE )

title: Get a filtered table of Gutenberg work metadata — gutenberg_works
text: ... Additional filters, given expressions using variables gutenberg_metadata dataset (e.g. author == "Austen, Jane") languages Vector languages include only_text Whether works must Gutenberg text attached. Works without text (e.g. audiobooks) downloaded gutenberg_download rights Values allow rights field. default allows public domain US "None", excluding works copyright. NULL allows value Rights distinct Whether return one distinct combination title gutenberg_author_id. multiple occur (fulfill conditions), uses one lowest ID all_languages Whether, multiple languages given, need present work. example, c("en", "fr") given, whether en/fr opposed English French works returned only_languages Whether exclude works languages besides ones provided. example, whether include en/fr English works requested

title: Get a filtered table of Gutenberg work metadata — gutenberg_works
text: tbl_df (see tibble dplyr packages) one row   work, format gutenberg_metadata.

title: Get a filtered table of Gutenberg work metadata — gutenberg_works
text: default, returns English-language works text format Gutenberg (opposed audio) Whose text copyright one distinct field title/author pair

title: Get a filtered table of Gutenberg work metadata — gutenberg_works
code: if (FALSE) { # interactive() # \donttest{ library(dplyr)  gutenberg_works()  # filter conditions gutenberg_works(author == "Shakespeare, William")  # language specifications  gutenberg_works(languages = "es") %>%   count(language, sort = TRUE)  gutenberg_works(languages = c("en", "es")) %>%   count(language, sort = TRUE)  gutenberg_works(languages = c("en", "es"), all_languages = TRUE) %>%   count(language, sort = TRUE)  gutenberg_works(languages = c("en", "es"), only_languages = FALSE) %>%   count(language, sort = TRUE) # } }

title: gutenbergr 0.2.2 (2022-12-03)
text: Updated metadata (#32, #29) minor bug fixes improvements, including removing broken url updating documentation comply CRAN roxygen2 requirements (#30, #31, #35, #28). Changed maintainer (#30)

title: gutenbergr 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2020-09-22 Changed comply CRAN policies API packages. Tests connect Project Gutenberg skipped CRAN, supplemented tests mock connection. Added gutenberg_languages dataset one-row-per-language-per-work, substantially speeds gutenberg_works. adds files argument gutenberg_download generally used testing. Made changes work dplyr 1.0.0, removing filter_ distinct_. Fixed links https

title: gutenbergr 0.1.5
text: CRAN release: 2019-09-10 Make compatible tidyr v1.0.0 data_frame deprecated, use tibble (thanks @evanodell #21) ROpenSci updates README (thanks @maelle #23)

title: gutenbergr 0.1.4
text: CRAN release: 2018-01-26 Added curl SUGGESTS, since ’s installed readr::read_lines fail

title: gutenbergr 0.1.3
text: CRAN release: 2017-06-19 Project Gutenberg mirror Maryland Public Libraries (http://www.gutenberg.lib.md.us) broken months. provided robot/harvest, replaces http://aleph.gutenberg.org. Changed test .zip capability run CRAN Removed rvest dependency

title: gutenbergr 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2016-06-24 Made compatible change distinct dplyr 0.5 (submitted CRAN) Removed xml2 dependency

title: gutenbergr 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2016-05-16 Transferred repo ownership ropenscilabs license changed MIT GPL-2. based realization catalog data licensed GPL, package includes processed version catalog data. (See ). Updated datasets 5/5/2016 added “date_updated” attribute tell last updated Added all_languages only_languages arguments gutenberg_works, allowing fine-grained control languages. (example, “either English French” “English French”) Changed get_gutenberg_mirror use xml2 directly, order handle AppVeyor Removed use data() gutenberg_works, since slows gutenberg_works 2X Various documentation, vignette, README adjustments response ROpenSci feedback. Added AppVeyor Windows continuous integration Added code coverage information codecov.io covr, along tests improve coverage

title: gutenbergr 0.1
text: CRAN release: 2016-05-03 gutenberg_download function, downloading one works Project Gutenberg using Gutenberg IDs Datasets Project Gutenberg metadata: gutenberg_metadata, gutenberg_subjects, gutenberg_authors gutenberg_works function retrieve filtered version gutenberg_metadata Introductory vignette including basic examples downloading books Unit tests gutenberg_download gutenberg_works Added NEWS.md file track changes package.
